author: canepat
content: This snapshot is relative to the integration with a [slightly old version](https://github.com/eth-act/zkevm-benchmark-workload/tree/d7181aa47ac050a1a831327a3720cabde86cc49e) of `eth-act/zkevm-benchmark-workload`, I'm now working at rebasing all the work on latest commit. That's why also the numbers reflect a previous version of benchmark tests, which selected 940 test fixtures (1077 in latest one)

author: developeruche
content:
This is really good, that's almost 2x... this is before flat-object input format impl right?

Have you guys dove to understand where these performance boosts are coming from? this would be of help to other teams!

<@745384946310578317> looking forward to the updated numbers when the rebase is done. Happy to help test when the followup PR is up 🙏

author: somnergy
content:
There is an inherent flaw in the input validation that's skipping the pre-trie validation. A naive addendum to do that should tank the performance somewhere between 20 and 40%. That's why I am working on recovering most of the cost by a better database format that's fully flat and very low mem alloc.
So, in a release in near future that would be doing pre, post and other extra state, trie, input validations. With the new flat format the overall tank to the performance should be a small <10% drop because of improved overall efficiency.

author: somnergy
content:
Where is the speed coming from? EVMOne is baseline quite a bit faster than revm, zilkworm is heavily optimized for SP1 hypercube.
The speed is coming from painfully curated good engineering at this point.If you so far as remove some optimizations with around 100 lines of code change one can possibly tank it 300% - simple as that.

author: somnergy
content: Partly, the speed is also coming from the pre-indexed input that we used and that speed is going to go even higher with the new format. But that part is maybe responsible for 10%-20%, it's a collection of 100 optimizations that get it to 100% speedup

author: developeruche
content: Awesome! looking forward to this release!!
